Schools closed news: All CBSE, ICSE, government, private schools to remain shut for 25-50 days in Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Chandigarh, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh due to…
Severe heatwave conditions are being witnessed across large parts of North and Central India. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned that heatwave conditions could become even more dangerous from mid-May to June. Temperatures have already crossed 45 degrees Celsius in several states, including Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Rajasthan, and Telangana.
Severe heatwave conditions are being witnessed across large parts of North and Central India. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned that heatwave conditions could become even more dangerous from mid-May to June. Temperatures have already crossed 45 degrees Celsius in several states, including Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Rajasthan, and Telangana.
Schools Closed Due to Extreme Heat
Due to scorching sunlight, severe heat, and dangerous hot winds, several state governments and district administrations have announced early and extended summer vacations for schools to ensure student safety. The orders apply to Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE), ICSE, government, and private schools. In areas where holidays have not yet started, schools are being shifted to morning timings to protect children from the afternoon heat.
Delhi-NCR and Uttar Pradesh Holiday Schedule
In Delhi, the Directorate of Education started summer vacations from May 11, 2026, and schools will reopen on July 1, 2026. In Uttar Pradesh, government schools will remain closed from May 20 to June 15, 2026. Many private schools have already started vacations from May 15 due to rising temperatures. In districts like Noida and Ghaziabad, schools are currently operating only from 7 AM to 12 PM.
Rajasthan and Haryana Announce Closures
According to the Rajasthan Education Department, schools in the state will remain closed from May 17 to June 20, 2026. In Haryana and Chandigarh, temperatures are continuously rising, and schools are expected to remain shut from June 1 to June 30, although holidays may begin earlier depending on weather alerts.
Bihar Summer Vacation Plan
Bihar is also preparing to close schools due to the heatwave. Summer vacations in the state are scheduled from June 1 to June 20, 2026, giving students a 20-day break during the peak summer season.
Southern and Other States Already on Break
In Telangana and Andhra Pradesh, summer vacations had already been announced from April 24 to June 11, 2026, because of extreme weather conditions. Schools in Chhattisgarh are closed from April 20 to June 15, while Odisha extended school holidays from April 27 onwards due to intense heatwave conditions.
Important Advice for Students and Parents
Students and parents are advised to regularly check with their schools for the latest updates on vacation schedules, as dates may change depending on local weather conditions and heatwave alerts.
